title: THE BOSS: sweep the tree
prompt: Everything you've learned, one move. Scattered across the lab's folders are .log files; some lines start with KEY=. Collect EVERY such line from EVERY .log file — and only .log files — sort them, and save them to keys.txt in the lab root. One of the collected lines holds the flag. New trick: grep -rh 'TEXT' . searches every file under here (-h hides filenames), and --include='*.log' limits the search to .log files.

checks.command: sort -C keys.txt && echo sorted || echo unsorted

details.restate: keys.txt must hold exactly the four KEY= lines that live in .log files (the one in the .txt decoy does NOT count), in sorted order. The KEY=delta line carries the flag — submit it too.

io.because: -r sweeps the whole tree, -h drops the filename prefixes, --include filters to .log files
pipe into sort, redirect into the file — the full toolchain in one line

help.plain: One pipeline: recursive grep limited to .log files, piped through sort, redirected into keys.txt.
help.analogy: A trawler sweep of the whole bay — the net (grep -r) covers everything, the mesh size (--include) decides what gets kept, and the catch gets sorted on deck.
help.mnemonic: search deep, keep only logs, sort, save: grep -rh … --include … | sort > file.
help.nudge: You need three tools chained: the recursive search you just learned, the sorter, and the redirect. What order do they go in?
